sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the current state after rotation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== Objects ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R', 'N', 'Y', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(r for r,c in b)
        maxr = max(r for r,c in b)
        minc = min(c for r,c in b)
        maxc = max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({minr},{maxr},{minc},{maxc})")

# Check ASCII around character
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Area around character ===")
for i in range(10, 45):
    line = ascii_lines[i][10:35]
    if any(c != 'O' for c in line):
        print(f"Row {i:2d}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I see the character has rotated. The white "P" is now at rows 26-34, cols 11-28. It's rotated 90° clockwise from the previous orientation.
Looking at the ASCII, the character is now:
- Rows 26-28: WWWWWWWWWWWWWWWWW (horizontal bar, 18 W's)
- Rows 29-31: RRR followed by OOO...WWW (red blocks on left, white on right)
- Rows 32-34: RRR followed by WWWWWWWWW (red blocks on left, white on right)
So the character is now a horizontal "P" shape pointing right. The red blocks are at the bottom-left of the character.
Looking at the layout:
- White "P" at rows 26-34, cols 11-28
- Yellow "P" at rows 35-52, cols 17-31
- Green bar at rows 14-34, cols 38-49
- Blue "U" at rows 53-58, cols 50-58
The white "P" is now above the yellow "P". The character's red blocks are at rows 29-31, cols 11-13 and rows 32-34, cols 17-19.
I think the goal is to position the white "P" character so that it connects with all the other objects (green bar, yellow "P", blue "U"). The red blocks seem to be "connection points" that indicate where the character needs to connect.
Let me think about this differently. In Level 1, the goal was to move the character to fit into the green rectangle's hole. In Level 2, maybe the goal is to move and rotate the character so that it connects with all the other objects.
Looking at the current layout:
- White "P" is at the top-left, above the yellow "P"
- Green bar is at the top-right
- Yellow "P" is in the middle
- Blue "U" is at the bottom-right
The white "P" needs to connect with the green bar, yellow "P", and blue "U". The red blocks indicate the connection points.
Let me try moving the white "P" RIGHT to approach the green bar.
